title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a family visit.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Is your cousin still coming this weekend? || Woman: Yes, but she arrives on Sunday now, not Saturday. Her train was too expensive on Saturday afternoon. || Man: Will you still go to the museum? || Woman: No, [8]we'll have lunch at home first, then take a walk round the lake if the weather is good[/8]. She wants a quiet day after travelling. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Is your cousin still coming this weekend?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but she arrives on Sunday now, not Saturday. Her train was too expensive on Saturday afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Will you still go to the museum?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: No, we'll have lunch at home first, then take a walk round the lake if the weather is good. She wants a quiet day after travelling. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: What does the woman plan to do with her cousin?
questions.explanation: The woman says they will have lunch at home and then walk round the lake if the weather is good.

extracts.context: You hear a man talking about learning to drive.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: I started driving lessons last month. At first I was most nervous about changing gear, but that is easier now. My instructor is calm, which helps. || Speaker: [9]The thing I still find hardest is parking, especially when there are cars waiting behind me[/9]. I need more practice before my test. Driving on main roads is not as frightening as I expected. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I started driving lessons last month. At first I was most nervous about changing gear, but that is easier now. My instructor is calm, which helps.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: The thing I still find hardest is parking, especially when there are cars waiting behind me. I need more practice before my test. Driving on main roads is not as frightening as I expected. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

questions.question: What does the man find most difficult now?
questions.explanation: He says the thing he still finds hardest is parking, especially with cars waiting behind him.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a school play.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: Are you going to audition for the school play? || Boy: I thought about acting, but I'm not good at remembering lines. || Girl: They need people backstage too. || Boy: That's what I'm interested in. [10]I'd like to help with the lights, because my brother showed me how they work at his theatre[/10]. Painting scenery sounds fun, but lights would teach me more. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.text: Are you going to audition for the school play?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I thought about acting, but I'm not good at remembering lines.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: They need people backstage too.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: That's what I'm interested in. I'd like to help with the lights, because my brother showed me how they work at his theatre. Painting scenery sounds fun, but lights would teach me more. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: How does the boy want to help with the play?
questions.explanation: The boy says he would like to help with the lights because his brother showed him how they work.

extracts.context: You hear two people talking about a flat.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you see the flat above the bakery? || Man: Yes. It was bright and the kitchen had new cupboards. The rent was lower than I expected too. || Woman: Sounds perfect. || Man: Almost. [11]The bedroom window faced the main road, and I could hear buses even with it closed[/11]. I need somewhere quieter because I work night shifts. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.text: Did you see the flat above the bakery?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes. It was bright and the kitchen had new cupboards. The rent was lower than I expected too.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Sounds perfect.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Almost. The bedroom window faced the main road, and I could hear buses even with it closed. I need somewhere quieter because I work night shifts. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: Why might the man not rent the flat?
questions.explanation: The man says the bedroom faces the main road and he can hear buses, so it may be too noisy.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking to a friend about a picnic.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are we still having the picnic in the park tomorrow? || Woman: Yes, but let's use the tables near the playground, not the grass by the river. || Man: Because of the wind? || Woman: Partly, but [12]my main reason is that my little niece is coming, and she can play nearby while we eat[/12]. I'll bring sandwiches, and Sam is bringing fruit. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are we still having the picnic in the park tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but let's use the tables near the playground, not the grass by the river.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Because of the wind?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Partly, but my main reason is that my little niece is coming, and she can play nearby while we eat. I'll bring sandwiches, and Sam is bringing fruit. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: Why does the woman prefer the tables near the playground?

options.C: they are closer to the river

questions.explanation: The woman says her little niece can play nearby while they eat.

extracts.context: You hear a teacher speaking to students before an exam.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: Before the test begins, please put your bags at the front of the room. You may keep a pen, pencil and eraser on your desk, but no phones or watches. || Speaker: [13]If you have a water bottle, remove the label and place it on the floor beside your chair[/13]. Do not open the question paper until I tell you to start. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Before the test begins, please put your bags at the front of the room. You may keep a pen, pencil and eraser on your desk, but no phones or watches.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: If you have a water bottle, remove the label and place it on the floor beside your chair. Do not open the question paper until I tell you to start. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

questions.question: What should students do with water bottles?
questions.explanation: The teacher says water bottles should have the label removed and be placed on the floor beside the chair.
